evidence_text: Agents like Claude Code, etc are amazing but mostly restrained to the CLI, while a ton of valuable work needs a browser. This is a fundamental limitation to what these agents can do. So far, attempts to add browsers to these agents (Claude’s built-in --chrome, Playwright MCP, agent-browser, etc.) all have interfaces that are unnatural for browsing.

As for the GPT-4 API, I don't use it extensively at the moment, just occasionally to experiment with newer applications like Microsoft's Jarvis project (HuggingGPT). Eventually, I plan to integrate the Whisper API, GPT-4 API, and Elevenlabs API to create my own personal assistant. For those without access, GPT-3.5 is still an impressive tool.
